title: Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: The Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary is a nature reserve in Mandi district, Himachal Pradesh, India, which covers 41.32 square kilometres (15.95 sq mi) and is located approximately seven
overview.description: The Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary is a nature reserve in Mandi district, Himachal Pradesh, India, which covers 41.32 square kilometres (15.95 sq mi) and is located approximately seven kilometres from the town of Sundar Nagar. The sanctuary extends along the east and south sides of the Seri Khad stream. "Khad" is the Himachali term for a stream that provides essential water sources for the sanctuary's plants and wildlife. The area is full of steep slopes, grasslands, and various nullahs.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kamaksha Temple
topPlaces.description: Shri Kamaksha Temple Jaidevi, also known as the Kul Devi (family deity) of the Suket royal family and the Raj-Rajeshwari and Adhisthatri Devi of the Suket principality, is a historic Hindu temple surrounded by mountains in a natural setting. Kamaksha means "the goddess who fulfills all desires." This place is famous in the region and is known as Jaidevi.

topPlaces.name: Kamaksha
topPlaces.description: Kamaksha means the goddess who fulfills all kinds of desires. Kamaksha is considered the embodiment of the Hindu goddess Mahishasuramardini.

topPlaces.name: Koldam Dam
topPlaces.description: The Koldam Hydropower Station, commonly known as Koldam, is an embankment dam on the Sutlej River upstream of the Dehar Power House. It is situated in Salaper, between two districts Bilaspur and Mandi, 18 km from Bilaspur off the Chandigarh-Manali Highway (NH-21) near Barmana, Himachal Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Suket Fair
topPlaces.description: Suket Fair is an annual Indian state-level fair held in Sundar Nagar, Himachal Pradesh during the Hindu festival Chaitra Navratri. According to the local people, it is also known by the names Suket Devta Mela and Sundernagar Mela, among others.

topPlaces.name: Sundar Nagar
topPlaces.description: Sundar Nagar (also spelled as Sundernagar) is a town and a municipal council in Mandi district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It was formerly a princely state, known as Suket.

topPlaces.name: Suket State
topPlaces.description: Suket State was one of the princely states of India during the period of the British Raj. The capital of the state was Pangna.

items.note: Shri Kamaksha Temple Jaidevi, also known as the Kul Devi (family deity) of the Suket royal family and the Raj-Rajeshwari and Adhisthatri Devi of the Suket principality, is a historic Hindu temple surrounded by mountains in a natural setting. Kamaksha means "the goddess who fulfills all desires." This place is famous in the region and is known as Jaidevi.
Kamaksha means the goddess who fulfills all kinds of desires. Kamaksha is considered the embodiment of the Hindu goddess Mahishasuramardini.

The Koldam Hydropower Station, commonly known as Koldam, is an embankment dam on the Sutlej River upstream of the Dehar Power House. It is situated in Salaper, between two districts Bilaspur and Mandi, 18 km from Bilaspur off the Chandigarh-Manali Highway (NH-21) near Barmana, Himachal Pradesh, India.

Suket Fair is an annual Indian state-level fair held in Sundar Nagar, Himachal Pradesh during the Hindu festival Chaitra Navratri. According to the local people, it is also known by the names Suket Devta Mela and Sundernagar Mela, among others.
Sundar Nagar (also spelled as Sundernagar) is a town and a municipal council in Mandi district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It was formerly a princely state, known as Suket.
Suket State was one of the princely states of India during the period of the British Raj. The capital of the state was Pangna.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (44 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (86 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bandli Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kamaksha Temple, Kamaksha, Koldam Dam, Suket Fair, Sundar Nagar.
